items.summary: DeviceRadixSort/radix_sort_keys drops & misorders keys when begin_bit>0 and end_bit==key width (gfx90a, ROCm 7.2.1)
cudaArrayLayered+SurfaceLoadStore array written layer-by-layer collapses its layer dimension on later-launch reads (gfx90a, ROCm 7.2.1)
Feature request: low-level cusolverSp sparse factorization API (csrqr*/csrchol*, csrlsvluHost, csrsymrcm) has no hipSOLVER path
ROCm 7.2.1 ships no libcu++ (<cuda/std/*>); ROCm/libhipcxx fills it. Revisit: gsplat 3DGUT path (needs cuda::std::optional) and the MPPI-Generic cuda::std->std workaround
Concluded NOT a ROCm bug: atomicMin/Max on fine-grained/remote memory needs system-scope atomics (usage requirement). Minor doc papercut could still be filed
NPP-based GPU image filters have no ROCm equivalent; ops report unsupported and the CPU/IPP path is used. A rocfft/rocAL or hand-written HIP image-filter path could restore GPU acceleration

Non-default SlabHash hashmap backend is wave64-incorrect (32-lane __ffs/__ballot election); compiles but tests excluded under USE_HIP. A wave64-correct rewrite would re-enable it (default StdGPU backend already works)
Environment-Gaussian reflection path not ported: diff-surfel-tracing uses OptiX (optixModuleCreate), which has no ROCm equivalent. Core 3DGS rasterization path works.
HIP-Vulkan texture interop stubbed (importToCuda mipmapped-array export): hipExternalMemoryGetMappedMipmappedArray is not exported by libamdhip64 in ROCm 7.2. Core rasterization works.
MoE path unsupported on AMD; fp16/bf16 and cublasLt-int8 Linear are supported.
GDS/cuFile (no ROCm equivalent) and nvjpeg GPU JPEG decode scoped out (CPU libjpeg fallback); nvimgcodec/cuslide2 not built.
Partial cudf port: many .cu omitted from cudf_hip_sources.cmake (full lists/strings/dictionary/structs, quantiles, scan_inclusive for strings/structs); core plus reductions/merge in scope.
Mixed-precision half2 device kernels not ported; mixed precision reports unsupported on HIP and is forced off.
moderngpu path (warp_size=32, inline PTX) not ported; k2/csrc/moderngpu_shim.h.
topk.cu CUB segmented-sort path not ported; Sort() falls back to an alternative.
Sparse (hipSPARSE generic-API) port deferred/stubbed off.
Hardware decompression path (no HIP equivalent) not ported.
lru_cache.cu (cuda::std::semaphore, no ROCm libcu++) not ported.
OpOSD, OpBndBox, OpBoxBlur (cuOSD 3rdparty) scoped out.

Only the core point-cloud/geometry slice (utility/camera/knn/geometry, CUPOCH_CORE_ONLY=ON) is built/validated on ROCm. Deferred GPU modules: registration (ICP/GICP/Colored/FGR/FilterReg), odometry, integration (TSDF/KinectFusion), collision, planning, kinfu, imageproc (libSGM stereo), io-beyond-geometry, visualization, Python module. Causes: (a) visualization/imageproc/io/pybind need OpenGL/GLEW/GLFW + libSGM + urdfdom host stack; (b) four geometry-factory .cu have strict-clang blockers re-enablable without OpenGL.

All ANN/vector-search algorithms deferred behind CUVS_HIP_SLICE=distance; only pairwise-distance + fused-NN (43/264 .cu, ~16%) is built/validated. Deferred: brute-force, IVF-Flat, IVF-PQ, CAGRA, NN-Descent, Vamana/DiskANN, ball_cover, HNSW interop, multi-GPU, plus cluster/selection/preprocessing/stats/embed and spectral/lanczos. completed status is scoped to the distance subsystem only.

neighbors/ ANN (CAGRA, IVF-Flat, IVF-PQ, NN-Descent, HNSW) and cluster/ (kmeans, kmeans_balanced, single_linkage) are header-only and were never compiled or GPU-validated under HIP; warp intrinsics + ~28 wave64 0xffffffff masks in detail/cagra and detail/ivf_pq need the faiss_select treatment. Reasonable to scope out (upstream deprecated these to cuVS, de-compiled after 24.12). Validated surface: faiss_select top-k, ball_cover, KNN brute force, EpsNeighRbc, rmat, header primitives.

Entire metrics module deferred though 10 cuvs-FREE metrics are buildable today (accuracy_score, adjusted_rand_index, completeness_score, entropy, homogeneity_score, kl_divergence, mutual_info_score, r2_score, rand_index, v_measure); plan option-a (split metrics target) not taken. Also deferred: dbscan/knn/tsne/umap/kmeans/hdbscan/svm/FIL/tree_shap/spectral/multi-GPU/Python and standalone hierarchy/linkage.cu. The 10 metrics are shippable now (raft headers present, no CUML_LINK_CUVS); shipping triggers gfx90a/gfx1100 revalidation.

Only the basic CUDA trainer (full_cuda_train_egg.cu) ported; 1 of 5 trainers, 0 advanced/distributed. Deferred: full_cuda_train_egg_transformer.cu + _adam.cu (EGG_WARP_SIZE=32 on off=16 shfl_down reductions, RoPE shfl_xor width-32); full_cuda_train_transformer_adam_mgpu.cu (Int8NativeFormer) + muon_internal.cuh (cuBLAS->hipBLAS Newton-Schulz + multi-GPU); d-eggs/ distributed coordinator/worker (hipGraph capture, managed-memory atomic audit).

waLBerla LB/EK GPU backend (60 generated CUDA.cu lattice-Boltzmann + electrokinetics + reaction kernels in src/walberla_bridge) scoped out via ESPRESSO_BUILD_WITH_WALBERLA=OFF. waLBerla is a separate upstream with its own CUDA gpu backend; porting it means HIP-ifying walberla::gpu itself.

ProstT5 GPU inference (--gpu 1) not enabled on ROCm: top-level CMakeLists sets GGML_CUDA from ENABLE_CUDA but never GGML_HIP from USE_HIP, so createdb --prostt5-model --gpu 1 silently falls back to CPU. ggml ships a vendored GGML_HIP backend (lib/prostt5/ggml/src/ggml-hip); a follow-up could wire USE_HIP->GGML_HIP and validate (needs rocBLAS/hipBLAS + weights, egress-bound). Structure-search GPU path IS delivered.

Eilmer GPU finite-rate chemistry (src/eilmer/cuda_gpu_chem.d driver + utils/alpha_qss_kernel_cuda_tmplt.cu, makefile target eilmer-cuda-gpu-chem under WITH_CUDA_GPU_CHEM) not ported; opt-in non-default separate binary, plain runtime API + one hand-written kernel (cublas imported but never called) so HIP-portable. chicken is the ported primary GPU path.

PyTorch extension mhc_cuda (primary advertised interface: setup.py CUDAExtension, consumed by mhc/ops.py + trainer) not ported to ROCm; hipify leaves cuBLASLt symbols un-renamed (cublasLtOrder_t, CUBLASLT_ORDER_ROW, CUBLASLT_MATRIX_LAYOUT_ORDER), needs const-void* hipFuncSetAttribute cast and the row-major to col-major hipBLASLt swap. Standalone CMake kernel harness validated on gfx90a/gfx1100 as the correctness gate.

Port covers only the cudaCoord (CUDA/Thrust/CUB) plugin. Excluded GPU surfaces: openaccPTM plugin (NVIDIA OpenACC offload via nvc++, the USEGPU path) -- no production ROCm OpenACC analogue; SAXS ArrayFire-CUDA backend (ArrayFire OpenCL could target AMD); core OpenACC infra (src/tools/OpenACC.h) and the libtorch-CUDA pytorch module. All opt-in/default-off.

GPU random number generator not enabled on HIP: include/visionaray/random_generator.h guards the thrust include and rand_engine device path under #if defined(__CUDACC__) only, so device RNG is unavailable under __HIPCC__. Small fix (extend both guards to accept __HIPCC__/__HIP_DEVICE_COMPILE__); needs GPU revalidation of any sampler path using it.

linux-gfx90a/gfx1100 marked completed but GPU validation exercised only 3 of 46 built SG algorithms (BFS, SSSP, PageRank); 44 linked SG test binaries never run on GPU. Unvalidated algorithms share the prim machinery where real wave64 bugs were found+fixed. Resolve by running the full in-scope SG gtest set from cugraph_hip_tests, or scope the completed claim to BFS/SSSP/PageRank.

Validation used a proxy microbenchmark; never exercised OCTproZ app kernels, GL-interop, or the 3D-volume surface-write path (riskiest ported feature compiled but not run) on real GPU. Re-validate by launching the built app through its OCT processing pipeline.
Recorded GPU validation exercised only the default 1NN_INDEX profile; the wave64 SUM_THRESH reduction fix is unvalidated. Validate the SUM_THRESH path against the reference oracle on gfx90a (wave64).

HIP validation gate omits built GPU kernels: tensor_nn_ops.cu (max_pool2d, adaptive_avg_pool2d, bias_relu, relu) has no test, and ~51 compute-only test files are excluded from LFS_HIP_TEST_FILES (beyond the 3 the validator caught). Add test_tensor_nn_ops.cpp and the compute-only tests to the gate.

build-hip src HEAD drifted one functional commit beyond the validated/recorded sha, undocumented and unvalidated. Reconcile status.json with the live tree (re-validate the drift or reset to the validated sha).

Comparison vs ROCm/hipCollections (canonical AMD port, ROCm-DS-owned): our net-new asset is sub-word int8/int16 atomic CAS emulation (include/cuco/detail/hip_subword_atomic.cuh) -- hipColl packed_cas handles only 4/8-byte and would corrupt int8/int16-keyed static_set/map (no small-key tests there). Secondary candidates: gfx1100/wave32 validation evidence to promote hipColl experimental wave32 + extend arch allowlist to RDNA4/Windows; newer-cuco features (roaring_bitmap, bloom merge/intersect, dynamic_map) via a cuco rebase. NOT contributions (hipColl already has them): pre-Volta __CUDA_ARCH__ guard byte-identical, bloom host-gate (theirs better). hipCollections is ROCm-DS-owned so DEFER PRs until ROCm-DS collaboration resumes (jeff 2026-06-07).

CDNA matrix cores DONE: TENSOR=ON ported to rocWMMA (native fp32 MFMA), gfx90a-validated (matches non-tensor docking; 1.57x faster at NUMWI=128), commit 3d466a4. REMAINING (follow-up): RDNA (gfx1100/1101/1201) has no native f32 WMMA input, so TENSOR=ON there needs a bf16 error-correction path (the AMD analog of the NVIDIA tf32+EC tcec). Currently CDNA-only; the Makefile should guard TENSOR=ON to CDNA archs.

rocPRIM DeviceScan lookback tile-state corrupts the scan non-deterministically at tile boundaries when its temp storage is not 256-byte aligned (CUB has no such requirement). catboost split_points.cu works around it by 256-aligning the scan temp offset. Genuine correctness bug; needs a minimal reproducer + rocm-libraries issue.

rocPRIM segmented radix sort leaves out-of-segment (gap) elements undefined in the alternate DoubleBuffer and flips Current() there (CUB leaves the result in-place); catboost segmented_sort.cu seeds both buffers to compensate. Clarify intended behavior vs CUB; possible deviation.

rocPRIM SortPairs needs more temp storage than a SortKeys-sized query allocates -> hipErrorInvalidValue at Run (CUB tolerates the keys-vs-pairs sizing mismatch). catboost segmented_sort.cpp sizes for SortPairs under USE_HIP. Partly a catboost CUB-contract mis-use; worth confirming rocPRIM's documented contract.

Real-scene garden fit faults at the LM step on gfx90a (MI250X, wave64). Root-caused to an off-by-one between the sparse-Jacobian EMIT kernel (gsgn.cu ~269-298) and forward.cu renderCUDA is_gaussian_hit: a boundary Gaussian the forward pass excludes via the transmittance terminator gets map_visible_gaussians=-1, so apply_jt_kernel reads geomBuffer[-1] -> HSA memory access fault on ROCm (benign in-chunk over-read on NVIDIA, which also zero-fills torch::empty). The emit kernel counts contributors with a wavefront WarpScan, so it MIGHT be wave64-specific. DIAGNOSTIC NEEDED on gfx1100 (wave32) to disambiguate: run a real Mip-NeRF360 garden fit (recipe + MOAT_PY MAPVIS_OOB probe in notes.md). If gfx1100 ALSO faults / MAPVIS_OOB -> the off-by-one is UPSTREAM (report to lukasHoel; ROCm just makes the latent over-read fatal). If gfx1100 completes cleanly -> WAVE64-SPECIFIC emit/WarpScan fix in the port. Both Linux + gfx1201 are already validated on synthetic scenes; this only affects real-scene fits.

gfx1201 SIGSEGV in hipblaslt_f8::is_inf via hipblasLtMatmulAlgoGetHeuristic for plain FP32 GEMM (FP8 type-check path runs unconditionally); 7.14.0a nightly regression 2026-06-07 -> 2026-06-08; workaround ROCBLAS_USE_HIPBLASLT=0. Filed upstream.

gfx90a ROCm 7.2.1: hipModuleLaunchKernel dispatches from inside HipModularProgram::launch()/launch2D memory-fault (addr nil, xnack-) while byte-identical inline sequences at the same call sites pass. Exhaustively bisected (args/Timer/launch API/module/order/sync/kernarg-lifetime/fast-math eliminated; machine code verified). Repro: CURAST_LADDER=1 on jeffdaily/CuRast branch bench. Workaround shipped: inline dispatch (INLINE_LAUNCH_1D/2D). Needs ROCm runtime-level debug (kernarg pool / queue state).
opencv dnn module CUDA backend (cuda4dnn/cuDNN) -> MIOpen backend: separate sizable project, out of cv::cuda port scope per jeff 2026-06-10 decision (plan.md open-decision-5)

cudalegacy graphcut/graphcut8 (NPP maxflow) has no ROCm analog; HIP takes the same stub path a modern CUDART>=8000 CUDA build does (NPP removed graphcut at 8.0). A GPU push-relabel/maxflow kernel could restore it later.
cudaimgproc NPP-only color/histogram ops not ported (untested upstream): alphaComp 13 Porter-Duff ops, 4ch histEven/histRange, 16U/16S histEven; gated StsNotImplemented on ROCm

cudaoptflow NvidiaOpticalFlow_1_0/2_0 hardware optical-flow classes gated off on ROCm (StsNotImplemented): AMD GPUs have no dedicated HW optical-flow engine and AMF exposes no optical-flow API. Public symbols kept; the software flows (Brox/SparsePyrLK/DensePyrLK/Farneback/TVL1) are the substitute and are ported+validated. Genuine HW feature loss.

cudacodec VideoWriter native hardware encode gated off on ROCm (StsNotImplemented): no ROCm-native encoder exists; AMF is a Vulkan/DX stack outside ROCm needing HIP<->Vulkan external-memory interop. Users are directed to the FFmpeg videoio backend (CAP_FFMPEG) with the AMF encoders h264_amf/hevc_amf/av1_amf. A native in-tree AMF encoder bridge is the deferred follow-up.

cudacodec VideoReader rocDecode decode implementation deferred to the RDNA followers. The WITH_ROCDECODE/HAVE_ROCDECODE CMake gates, FFmpeg-only demuxer wiring, and graceful decode-off runtime errors are in place and the module build-validates on gfx90a with decode off. The full cuvid->rocDec API mirror (video_decoder/parser/reader, CUVID struct translation, ColorSpace NV12->RGB pipeline activation) is NOT written/validated because gfx90a (MI250X, CDNA2) has no VCN video-decode engine and the ROCm-repo mesa-amdgpu-va-drivers needed by the rocDecode runtime is uninstallable on this headless node, so neither compile-against-runtime nor GPU validation is possible here. Implement + validate on gfx1100/gfx1201 (which carry VCN). rocDecode lacks MPEG-1/2, VC-1, VP8, MJPEG vs NVDEC: those are genuine codec losses to skip/StsUnsupported cleanly.

Three perf/coverage paths scoped out of the W4A16 ROCm port and guarded with throwing stubs: (1) tensor-core W4A16 GEMM (mma.sync MMA path) needs an MFMA/rocWMMA rewrite -- a portable SIMT GEMM (gemm_simt.cuh) is shipped instead; (2) W8A8 int8 GEMM (cvt/dp4a path) not ported; (3) FasterTransformer masked multi-head attention (single_query_attention) bound as a throwing stub. The validated path (gemv/gemm_forward_cuda_new via WQLinear/qmodule.py) is unaffected.
